infrastructure_findings.description: DISK AT 81% USED — crossed Mode 2 WARNING threshold. 1861MB free on 9.6GB partition. Filesystem is /dev/nvme0n1p1 / — the sandbox system volume, not the FUSE workspace (which is only 61MB). Agent clones accumulate on system volume. Per dispatcher: 'one bad run from full'. Not yet critical (<90%) but trending closer.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: df -h /: 9.6G total, 7.7G used, 1.9G avail, 81% used. /tmp is only 3.1M. /sessions is 61M. The 7.7G is primarily the base system image, not agent artifacts — so recovery options are limited to (a) clearing /tmp scratch files, (b) cleaning any agent's local clone directories, (c) accepting the baseline.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: Not self-fixable from tinker run (sandbox is per-agent). Recommend decider/curmudgeon/analyst audit their scratch clones after each run — if any leave /tmp files behind, clean them. Also worth verifying workspace-sync.md cleans up its temporary clone. If disk trend continues upward, will escalate to critical in subsequent reports.

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE STALE CARRYOVER: build-scripts/digest-reviews.js mismatched between workspace (md5 0a11b762) and GitHub main (md5 cda50957). Same file, same hashes as yesterday's report — NOT yet auto-fixed by a publish cycle. This is 2nd consecutive report with this exact mismatch. Any agent reading digest-reviews.js from workspace will run stale code.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5sum workspace: 0a11b76276b71343d3b356f06aed0e98. Curl GitHub raw: cda50957ede0772d66bf0e5b8e448313. Unchanged since 2026-04-13T16:11.
infrastructure_findings.suggested_fix: Decider or human: run `cp` from fresh clone to workspace, OR trigger a `node build.js publish` which has the workspace-sync step. The digest-reviews.js is used by the decider when pulling curmudgeon reviews — if the decider clones fresh each run (per freshness protection), the stale workspace copy doesn't corrupt its operation. Still worth fixing for consistency.

accuracy_findings.issue: Monitoring Pipeline table in CLAUDE.md documents BAU schedules. Actual state is churn-and-burn with schedules that rotate. Not a documentation error — CLAUDE.md correctly documents the canonical BAU. The discrepancy belongs to priority-queue.json metadata, not CLAUDE.md.

notes_to_future_tinker: Observations trending toward BAU transition: open_issues 32→7, queue 15→13. When BAU restores, watch for: (1) Step E4 bug will again produce metadata divergence; (2) PROP-005 waste rate will spike as no-op decider runs resume. If BAU restore happens before next tinker run, the first priority next run becomes verifying metadata-vs-reality converged. Also: FUSE stale digest-reviews.js is on day 2 without resolution — if it persists another cycle, escalate to major severity and manually cp. Consider filing a combined PROP bundling Step E4 + PROP-005 + decider.md schedule descriptions as a single 'decider hygiene' patch — the sequence of 4-8 consecutive reports without movement on these items suggests implementation friction benefits from bundling.
